original_image_tags: A line graph with a grid background. The y-axis is labeled "Mode-5 Constant A" and ranges from 2.5 to 6.5 in increments of 0.5. The x-axis is labeled "Breakup q-alpha (deg-lb/ft²)" and ranges from 0 to 25,000 in increments of 5,000. There are five downward-sloping lines on the graph, each labeled with a different B value. From top to bottom, the lines are labeled: "B = 5,000,000", "B = 500,000", "B = 100,000", "B = 50,000", and "B = 1,000". Near the bottom left of the graph, the text "Atlas IIAS" is present. A vertical dashed line is drawn at an x-axis value of approximately 7,500.

elements.description: Line graph titled 'Figure 14. Effects of Breakup q-alpha on A for Atlas IIAS'. The Y-axis is labeled 'Mode-5 Constant A' (ranging from 2.5 to 6.5) and the X-axis is labeled 'Breakup q-alpha (deg-lb/ft^2)' (ranging from 0 to 25000). The graph plots five distinct dashed lines labeled 'B = 5,000,000', 'B = 500,000', 'B = 100,000', 'B = 50,000', and 'B = 1,000'. The text 'Atlas IIAS' is printed near the bottom left of the plotting area.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content of the line graph (axes, ranges, five labeled lines, 'Atlas IIAS' text), but Description A adds a detail (vertical dashed line at x ≈ 7,500) that Description B omits.
